title: Release Automation, npm Trusted Publishing, and Change Control · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Build Once Promote Many
summary: A release is triggered from an unreviewed tag, rebuilds after approval, publishes npm with a long-lived token, mixes changelog generation with mutation, and cannot prove package or image provenance. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci build once promote many increment in multi-artifact release and publication policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Build Once Promote Many · Theory Ci Build Once Promote Many

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example tag rebuild publishes npm and images with long-lived tokens, missing provenance, and one-person approval. Build once, promote immutable artifacts, use trusted publishing, verify contents, and separate duties. Competency probe: Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds. A defensible solution applies Ci Build Once Promote Many, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-artifact release and publication policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-build-once-promote-many — Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-2
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-releases-packages-change-control-2
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: worked cd releases packages change control 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      source: protected-reviewed-tag
      checks: required-at-source-sha
      build: once
      approval: promote-existing-artifact
      image: immutable-digest
      npm: trusted-publishing
      npm-minimum: 11.5.1
      node-minimum: 22.14.0
      id-token: release-job-only
      package-provenance: verified
      separation: author-reviewer-builder-approver-publisher
    verify: compare tag authority source checks artifact digest package contents trusted publisher identity provenance approval and published result
    failure: Reject tag-only authorization, per-environment rebuild, long-lived publish token, OIDC for private install, provenance as benign-code proof, or approval count as safety.
    repair: Protect source authority, build once, approve promotion, publish through exact OIDC identity, verify package provenance, and separate duties by risk.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
